H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Strongest HEALING protocol (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78), excelling in overstimulation contexts. Qualitative data underscores calming effects, yet YouTube reach (30 views) lags behind efficacy.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners find this very calming, especially in overwhelming situations.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Consistent performance (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73) with 10-minute settlement. Neutral music reception highlights room for audio texture optimization.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Helps reduce overstimulation, but the music divides opinions.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Meaningful efficacy (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68), effective in sleep/activity contexts. Low YouTube views (8) hinder broader validation.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Effective for sleep and focus, but takes a few minutes to kick in.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: This protocol demonstrates strong efficacy (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93), with most listeners settling within 10 minutes and reporting positive outcomes. YouTube data shows minimal reach (7 views), indicating discovery potential. AutoGluon's predicted score (7.84) aligns with observed means, reinforcing its value.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: This protocol helps many listeners feel calmer within about 10 minutes, though not everyone finds it equally soothing.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62) with faster settlement (5 minutes). Qualitative responses show moderate improvements, though music reception is mixed. low YouTube views (6) highlight underexposure.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Most people notice some improvement, but the music isn’t for everyone.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Comparable efficacy to siblings (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89) but flags subgroup variance in outcomes. Qualitative data notes some non-responders, suggesting need for tailored variants.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Helps many settle quickly, but a few find it doesn’t work well for them.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Structurally expected lower scores (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70) reflect crisis-state listeners. Qualitative reports of significant improvements contrast with low mean, indicating complex response patterns.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Designed for intense states, it offers relief for many though some listeners struggle to engage.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Meaningful performance (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70) consistent with crisis protocols. Settle time variability (5-10 minutes) requires further exploration.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Stabilizes emotions for some, but results vary widely.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Developing confidence (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91) with rapid settlement (1-2 minutes). Mixed music/rhythm opinions suggest design refinements for broader appeal.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Works fast for some, others find it less effective.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: High-efficacy sleep protocol (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86) with quick settlement. YouTube data (4 views) indicates untapped potential.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Many find this protocol helps prepare for rest, though a few remain restless.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Steady performance (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55), though some non-effect reports suggest ceiling effects. YouTube reach (5 views) remains limited.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Gentle and soothing for some, others find it unremarkable.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Emerging signal (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68), effective yet underrepresented in data. Requires expanded listener base.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Promising for sleep support, but needs more listener feedback.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Low mean (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82) contrasts with THRIVING peers. Qualitative reports of distraction suggest design tweaks.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Helps some concentrate, others find it unhelpful.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Improved performance (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60) with concentrated benefits. YouTube views (4) limit reach analysis.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Effective focus aid, though not universally loved.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Top THRIVING protocol (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97), validated by AutoGluon. YouTube views (15) remain insufficient.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Highly rated for clarity, deserves wider attention.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Basic efficacy (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73) with room for improvement. Cross-bucket potential with mood/energy protocols warranting exploration.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Supports focus for some, others feel distracted.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Modest performance (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41) and low YouTube reach (2 views). Qualitative data highlights concentration benefits.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Boosts creativity for many, though engagement varies.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: High-potential variant (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32) with development status. Larger sample could confirm efficacy.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Shows strong potential, but limited feedback so far.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stable maintenance protocol (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34) with analytic drift concerns. Review parameter consistency.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stabilizes mood for many, though some notice little effect.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Variable responses (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03) and drift signals require stability checks. Qualitative data shows mixed reception.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed results – some benefit, others don’t.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: High mean (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73) contrasts with drift instability. Urgent need for cohort analysis.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Consistent mood/energy support (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94) with YouTube underexposure (8 views). Enhance discovery pathways.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Boosts energy gently, though not for everyone.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Steady performance (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71) and therapeutic alignment. Qualitative data highlights relaxation benefits.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Uplifting for many, with room to improve.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Mixed reception (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74) signals design complexity. Refine rhythmic elements for clarity.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Mixed performance (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01) with motivational challenges. Adjust frequency parameters for efficacy.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Small sample (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63) limits conclusions. Qualitative data suggests partial engagement.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Early results are mixed – stay cautious.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Promising variant (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38) warrants replication study. Expand listener recruitment.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Encouraging signs, but too few listeners to confirm.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Moderate efficacy (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62) with grounding effects reported. YouTube views (8) underrepresent potential.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Grounding for many, but not a quick fix.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Developing confidence (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64) with positive qualitative trends. Requires larger sample.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Shows promise for mental clarity, needs more feedback.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Low mean/high variance (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45) demands immediate redesign. Investigate subgroup mismatch.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Struggles to engage listeners – redesign recommended.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The data indicates a stable foundation across HEALING protocols, with emerging patterns in THRIVING and transformative challenges in TRANSFORMING.
• HEALING protocols consistently show high efficacy (mean ~7.0) with minimal variance, affirming their foundational role.
• THRIVING protocols exhibit variable performance, with standout exceptions like T-FC-L3 (7.63 mean), suggesting refinement opportunities.
• TRANSFORMING protocols face engagement challenges but display high potential in specific subgroups.
• YouTube reach remains underutilized, with several high-efficacy protocols lacking visibility.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series reflect distinct design intents through their efficacy profiles.
• HEALING protocols demonstrate rapid entrainment and broad applicability, as seen in H-OS-L1’s strong showing (mean=7.15).
• THRIVING protocols require nuanced engagement, with T-FC-L3’s success highlighting the importance of precise frequency modulation.
• TRANSFORMING protocols’ wide variance underscores the complexity of their target states, necessitating adaptive delivery.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Key anomalies warrant targeted investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (mean=5.7) shows unexpected underperformance, diverging from its series peers — possible subgroup mismatch.
• T-MB-L3’s analytic drift (similarity=0.557) signals evolving listener responses, requiring version-control review.
• H-CR-L1’s low mean (6.35) aligns with crisis-state expectations but contrasts with qualitative reports of significant improvement.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Actionable signals guide protocol refinement.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-OS-L1 (n=95, mean=7.15): Amplify YouTube promotion to match efficacy — explore platform-specific teasers.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7): Create L2 variant with adjusted harmonic structures to address potential mismatch.
• T-FC-L3 (n=35, mean=7.63): Cross-bucket to motivation protocols, adjusting Adviser routing for broader discovery.
• T-MB series: Stabilize hold-phase parameters to mitigate drift.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals both stability and evolution in protocol framing.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: Notable instability in T-MB-L3/T-MB-L1 (~0.56 similarity) suggests re-evaluating cohort shifts.
• SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Maintenance protocols (T-MB-L2/L3) form a coherent group, implying shared design efficacy.
• Confidence: Sufficient historical data supports drift analysis, but semantic clustering remains exploratory.
